libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Create and stop an in-process local HTTP endpoint in the generated tests, preferably JDK com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpServer bound to 127.0.0.1 on a random port, then exercise Client.create(), WebResource, GET/POST, headers, ClientResponse, and filters against that endpoint. No environment variables or system properties are required. Prefer text/plain, application/x-www-form-urlencoded, headers, and status-code coverage with the default URLConnection connector.
risks: Documentation examples often show JSON/JAXB entity mapping; those paths may require optional Jersey JSON/JAXB dependencies that are not transitively supplied by com.sun.jersey:jersey-client:1.9, especially on JDK 21.
Avoid Apache HTTP client connector coverage unless an explicit optional connector dependency is added; the default Client.create() path uses HttpURLConnection and needs no extra dependency.
Do not rely on public external HTTP services; use a local in-process server for deterministic Gradle and Native Image verification.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Jersey Client 1.9 is an HTTP REST client; meaningful coverage needs an actual HTTP endpoint, but the resolved artifact only requires jersey-core and the default connector uses JDK HttpURLConnection, so no extra Maven dependency or Docker service is needed.
